questions.question: Before reading: a pretrained language model has a body with 5M parameters and an output head that projects to a 30K vocabulary. You want a 2-class classifier. Which is the most defensible first move?
options: Discard the body and train a new transformer from scratch on the 800-example labelled set.
Replace only the head with a 2-class linear layer and decide later whether to train the body.
Keep the original head but mask all but two output logits.
Freeze the head and train only the body.
questions.explanation: The body is the expensive part and encodes useful structure from pretraining. Swap the head first. Whether to freeze the body is a separate decision driven by data size and domain drift.
questions.question_zh: 在阅读课程之前: 一个预训练 LM 的 body 有 5M 参数,output head 投影到 30K 词表。你想要一个 2 类分类器。最稳妥的第一步是?
options_zh: 丢掉 body,在 800 个标注样本上从头训练一个新的 transformer。
只换掉 head 为 2 类的 linear 层,以后再决定是否训练 body。
保留原 head,但把除两个 logit 外的全部 mask 掉。
冻结 head,只训练 body。
questions.explanation_zh: body 是昂贵且承载预训练结构的部分。先把 head 换掉。是否冻结 body 是另一个由数据规模和领域漂移驱动的决定。

questions.question: Why does the lesson use mean pooling with an attention-mask weighting instead of a plain mean across the sequence axis?
options: Plain mean is mathematically undefined for transformers.
Padding positions contribute irrelevant hidden states that would pull the pooled vector toward zero.
Mean pool requires masking by convention even when there are no pads.
Plain mean ignores the batch dimension.
questions.explanation: Padding tokens carry no signal but the body still produces hidden states for them. Without masking, padded positions corrupt the pooled representation, especially on short sequences in a batch padded to the longest example.
questions.question_zh: 为什么本节用带 attention-mask 的 mean pooling,而不是直接在序列轴上取平均?
options_zh: 对 transformer 来说普通平均在数学上未定义。
Padding 位置贡献无意义的隐藏状态,会把池化向量拉向 0。
即使没有 padding,mean pool 也按惯例需要 mask。
普通平均忽略 batch 维度。
questions.explanation_zh: Padding token 不携带信号,但 body 仍会为它们产生隐藏状态。不做 mask,padded 位置会污染池化表示,在批次中含短序列时尤其明显。

questions.question: In `train_classifier`, the optimiser is constructed with `[p for p in model.parameters() if p.requires_grad]`. What does this idiom enforce?
options: It silently ignores the head when the body is frozen.
It tells PyTorch to autograd through frozen parameters anyway.
It builds the param group from only the trainable subset, so the head-only and full-FT cases share the same code path.
It is required by the Adam constructor.
questions.explanation: The optimiser only updates the parameters in its param list. By filtering on `requires_grad`, the same training function works for both regimes; the toggle is the freeze call, not the loop.
questions.question_zh: 在 train_classifier 中,优化器以 [p for p in model.parameters() if p.requires_grad] 构造。这个写法保证了什么?
options_zh: 在 body 被冻结时静默忽略 head。
告诉 PyTorch 也对冻结参数 autograd。
只用可训练子集构造参数组,使 head-only 与 full-FT 共用同一段代码。
Adam 构造器要求这样做。
questions.explanation_zh: 优化器只更新参数列表里的参数。按 requires_grad 过滤,同一个训练函数就同时支持两种 regime; 切换的是 freeze,不是循环。

questions.question: Precision is 0.9 and recall is 0.6 on the spam class. What is the F1?
questions.explanation: F1 is the harmonic mean of precision and recall: 2 * 0.9 * 0.6 / (0.9 + 0.6) = 1.08 / 1.5 = 0.72.
questions.question_zh: 在 spam 类上 precision 是 0.9,recall 是 0.6,F1 是多少?

questions.question: You retrain the model with full fine-tuning at the same learning rate as head-only training. F1 collapses to 0.55 from 0.90. What is the most likely cause?
options: The body parameters needed a smaller learning rate than the head.
Adam is incompatible with classification objectives.
Cross-entropy loss is not defined for two-class problems.
PyTorch silently disables gradients during full fine-tuning.
questions.explanation: A pretrained body usually needs a smaller learning rate than a fresh head. A common pattern is two parameter groups: high LR on the head, smaller LR on the body. The same LR for both destroys pretraining signal.
questions.question_zh: 你以与 head-only 训练相同的学习率做全量 fine-tuning,F1 从 0.90 跌到 0.55。最可能的原因是?
options_zh: body 参数需要比 head 更小的学习率。
Adam 与分类目标不兼容。
Cross-entropy loss 在两类问题上未定义。
PyTorch 在全量 FT 时静默禁用了梯度。
questions.explanation_zh: 预训练 body 通常需要比新 head 更小的学习率。常见做法是两个参数组: head 用大 LR、body 用小 LR。两者同 LR 会破坏预训练信号。

questions.question: Your team wants to deploy the classifier and asks how to record which regime produced the production checkpoint. Which artifact answers that question reproducibly?
options: The final loss value, since head-only loss is always higher than full-FT loss.
The `trainable` count in `TrainReport`, which differs by orders of magnitude between regimes.
The confusion matrix, which uniquely identifies the regime.
The precision-recall-F1 triple.
questions.explanation: `trainable` records the number of parameters that received gradients during training. Head-only has on the order of `hidden * num_classes`; full FT has the whole model. The number is the audit trail.
questions.question_zh: 团队想部署该分类器,问如何可复现地记录是哪种 regime 产出的生产 checkpoint。哪份 artifact 能回答?
options_zh: 最终 loss 值,因为 head-only loss 总比 full-FT loss 高。
TrainReport 中的 trainable 参数计数,在两种 regime 间相差数量级。
混淆矩阵,它能唯一标识 regime。
precision-recall-F1 三元组。
questions.explanation_zh: trainable 记录训练中接收过梯度的参数数量。head-only 在 hidden × num_classes 量级; 全量 FT 是整模型。这个数字就是审计追溯。
